MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. P. BOBBY SHUKLA OF THE SHUKLA LAW, TEL. NO. 1-415-986-1338 EXT. 138 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. EMILY A. NUGENT & ATTY. KATHRYN BURKETT DICKSON OF THE DICKSON GEESMAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-510-899-4670 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SHAUNA N. CORREIA OF THE WEINTRAUB TOBIN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-772-9655 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.
RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Mercy High School's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the first, second and third causes of action for violation of FEHA and wrongful termination in violation of public policy in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Julie Ortiz is granted as to all three causes of action with leave to amend as to all three causes of action. Mercy High School's request for judicial notice is granted. Because Mercy High School is a religious nonprofit corporation, not a nonprofit public benefit corporation, it is not an employer per Government Code 12926(d) and is not subject to Government Code 12926.2(f). (Henry v. Red Hill Evangelical Tustin (2011) 201 Cal. App. 4th 1041, 1049-50). However, because it appears reasonably possible that Ms. Ortiz may be able to file an amended complaint which could state cognizable FEHA and wrongful termination in violation of public policy claims against Mercy High School, Ms. Ortiz is granted leave to file a second amended complaint to seek to do so. Ms. Ortiz' citation to an unpublished Washington federal court decision suggests that she might be able to allege that Mercy High School is estopped from invoking the language in section 12926(d) excluding religious corporations from the definition of an employer and dictum in Henry suggests that she may be able to tether her Tameny claim to the federal statute prohibiting age discrimination by employers. The court does not rule on whether Ms. Ortiz would be successful if she alleges these or other matters in a second amended complaint. If Ms. Ortiz plans to file a second amended complaint, she must do so by 4pm on May 4, 2017 if she wishes to maintain the trial date of May 8, 2017 and, if she does so, Mercy High School must file its response to the second amended complaint by 4pm on May 5, 2017. Under this time frame, any pleading or other issues raised by the second amended complaint and the response thereto must be addressed to the trial judge. If Ms. Ortiz plans to file a second amended complaint by May 19 and is agreeable to a continuance of the trial, to Nov. 13, 2017 and a schedule for filing the second amended complaint and the response thereto (including a briefing schedule and hearing date if the response is a demurrer or motion).
